THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT APPELLANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO TAKE NOTHING FROM RESPONDENT BANKSTON, DUBLIN THE JUDGMENT BELOW IS REVERSED, AND JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND AGAINST DUBLIN BANKSTON. THE JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL AND REPLACES THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF APRIL 11, 2019. THE PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED TO THE DESTRUCTION OF THE EXHIBITS ADMITTED DURING THIS APPEAL. THE CASE IS REMANDED BACK TO SMALL CLAIMS COURT. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR COMPLETE DETAILS. PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND CERTIFICATE OF MAILING FILED

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-03-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 611

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-05-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jude Pond, Esq. for Plaintiff/Respondent Dublin Bankston, who is also present.
131 Steuart Street, Suite #400 San Francisco, CA. 94105 Tel. (415) 543-9444 x236

Kaitlyn M. Murphy, Esq. for Defendant/Appellant City and County of San Francisco with witnesses Allison Mickels, Robert Milton and Elisa Della-Piana.
1390 Market Street, 6th Floor San Francisco, CA. 94102 (415) 554-3867.

This case comes on regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Dublin Bankston, plaintiff/respondent testifies on his own behalf. The following documentary evidence is introduced:

Exhibit 1 - Color photograph of 1401 Illinois St, facing north (intersection)
Exhibit 2 - Color photograph of 708 24th Street, facing east (intersection)
Exhibit 3 - Color photograph of 701 24th Street, facing east (intersection)
Exhibit 4 - Color photograph of 1399 Illinois Street, facing south (intersection)
Exhibit 5 - Color photograph of 699 24th Street facing west (intersection)
Exhibit 6 - Color photograph of 1399 Illinois Street, facing south (intersection)

Allison Mickels, on behalf of the City and County of San Francisco, testifes. The following documentary evidence is introduced:

Exhibit 7: Policy and procedure for public works property
EXhibit 8: Homeless property information slip
Exhibit 9: Photograph of bag and tag
Exhibit 10: Photograph of bikes
Exhibit 11: Photograph of items container
Exhibit 12: Photograph of personal property container

Plaintiff's Exhibit I is also referenced from her declaration.

Witnesses Robert Milton and Elisa Della-Piana also testify on behalf of Defendant/Appellant, the City and County of San Francisco.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the matter is under submission. A decision will be sent to the parties by next week.

By stipulation of counsel and the parties, all exhibits will be destroyed after the Court issues his ruling.

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: R. Gumpal, not reported (611).
